instruction.title: Operating Instructions
care: Do not feed after midnight (will start buzzing and asking for karaoke).
Do not give energy drinks (will switch to 200 BPM and won't stop).
Do not leave near AI (might catch a case of soullessness).
Do not bring to a mirror (will fix imaginary bangs for 2 hours).
Do not play cheap pop nearby (causes instant depression and beat indigestion).
Do not hit the casing (sincerity settings will reset).
Do not store in dusty playlists (gets covered in digital mold).
Do not let them hear your 2010 demos (will start crying uncontrollably).
Do not expose to direct sunlight (melancholy will fade).
Do not force to sing in the morning (voice sounds like an ungreased door).
Do not scare with sudden notifications (will lose the rhythm).
Do not feed cheap dumplings (lyrics become too mundane).
Do not leave alone with a guitar (might record a 40-minute solo).
Do not force to rhyme 'love' and 'dove' (causes creative paralysis).
Do not let them read YouTube comments (self-esteem and volume will drop).
Do not turn on a metronome (will start twitching nervously to the beat).
Do not store near mainstream hits (might lose individuality).
Do not give credit card access (will immediately buy a useless $500 plugin).
Do not let on stage without coffee (will forget lyrics even in an instrumental).
Do not play on bad speakers (gets offended and hides in the subwoofer).
dont-mix: Don't put in a playlist with cheap pop (possible critical software conflict and loss of dignity).
Don't play immediately after Beethoven (the artist will develop an inferiority complex).
Don't mix with construction site sounds (the listener won't notice the transition).
Don't play before sermons (causes cognitive dissonance and an urge to sin).
Don't play in front of tube-amp audiophiles (they'll find noise where there is none).
Don't queue after mumble rappers (the artist will also stop pronouncing letters).
Don't mix with white noise (the artist might dissolve in it forever).
Don't put in a 'Chanson of the Year' playlist (the guitar might spontaneously detune).
Don't play after 'How to become a millionaire' courses (the beat will start sounding too poor).
Don't put next to AC/DC (the artist will look too fragile and delicate).
Don't mix with children's lullabies (causes existential dread in listeners).
Don't put in a playlist for your exes (the track will start auto-deleting from pain).
Don't play after a 10-hour version of 'Epic Sax Guy' (nothing can save you then).
Don't play before your boss's speech (might set a too liberal tone).
Don't mix with jazz improvisation (the artist will get lost in three chords).
Don't play in $2 wireless headphones (the artist will be offended by the connection quality).
Don't put in a 'Workout Music' playlist (he's too lazy for that).
Don't mix with fungal cream commercials (ruins the whole vibe and mystery).
Don't play after Kanye West (the artist will start demanding a private jet).
Don't play in a karaoke bar (someone will definitely try to sing along and ruin everything).
love: Love him: he's very sensitive to volume levels (starts hiccuping at peaks).
Love him: at his age, other tracks have long since become alarm ringtones.
Love him: he's the only one who won't judge you for your guilty pleasures.
Love him: he's afraid of the dark in inactive browser tabs.
Love him: he spent three months writing this chorus on a diet of instant noodles.
Love him: he doesn't know how to lie; all his feelings are out on the equalizer.
Love him: he dreams of one day sounding from a real vinyl player.
Love him: he's very vulnerable when skipped in the first second.
Love him: he's the only one ready to be the soundtrack to your Saturday cleaning.
Love him: he remembers the times when music was sent via infrared port.
Love him: he considers your headphones his only home.
Love him: he specifically paused before the drop so you could take a breath.
Love him: he doesn't ask for money, he only asks for a bit of your attention.
Love him: he promised his mom he'd be a star (don't let him down).
Love him: he sounds so sincere, as if he has no autotune (he really doesn't).
Love him: he's ready to repeat infinitely if you press Loop.
Love him: he's quiet, humble, and will never interrupt your inner critic.
Love him: he's seen better days in the Downloads folder, but he chose you.
Love him: he still believes Kanye West will hear him.
Love him: he believes someone will definitely fall in love to his beat.
promo: Walk them in stories at least once a week (otherwise they'll start posting memes themselves).
Walk them under morning coffee videos (they love the smell of Arabica).
Walk them in Reels: they really want to see the world, even if only through your lens.
Walk them carefully: do not apply the 'dog ears' filter without consent.
Walk them in Shorts under cat compilations—it's their natural habitat.
Walk them on TikTok: they dream of a challenge, even if it's just standing still.
Walk them only in good lighting (they're shy about their low frequencies).
Walk them under the text 'This is my vibe': they truly believe it's true.
Walk them in gym stories: let them think they're buff.
Walk them in a Telegram channel: they like to feel elite.
Walk them at midnight under aesthetic city lights.
Walk them under unboxing videos: they love the rustle of paper.
Walk them in Q&A stories to fill those awkward silences.
Walk them against a sunset: it's their only chance to feel like they're in a music video.
Walk them under cooking videos: they dream of being the soundtrack to the perfect omelet.
Walk them in stories captioned 'On repeat': it's the best vitamin for them.
Walk them under travel montages: they want to believe they're a citizen of the world.
Walk them in 'Close Friends': they love gossip and secrets.
Walk them under rain videos: they pair perfectly with droplets on glass.
Walk them in stories with your cat: cats are the best producers, and they know it.

export.title: GUARDIAN CERTIFICATE
export.description-1: This is to certify that this
export.description-2: artist has been officially placed in kind hands.
export.name: Nickname
export.pledge-1: The guardian pledges to feed the artist with views and not
export.pledge-2: apply cringey filters. With love, Artist Shelter.

descriptions: Was pushed out of the 'Sleep Music' playlist by vacuum cleaner noise generated by AI. Very easygoing, requires no mastering.
His beat was stolen by AI and resold as 'Relaxing Lo-Fi for Hamsters'. Bites slightly when he sees ChatGPT.
A bard whose only listener is a ficus in the hallway. Dreams of a Shorts tour.
Old school. Sometimes howls at search engine algorithms.
Quiet, house-trained, and accustomed to background study music.
Very loud. Requires attention and lots of memes in stories.
His track is perfect for videos where someone falls epicly. Very self-ironic.
Lost his job at a coffee shop when AI learned to generate infinite jazz. Loves soy lattes and silence.
Deleted by an algorithm for 'too human vocals'. Looking for a home where fake notes and sincerity are valued.
Deepfake victim: AI stole his timbre and forced him to sing a borscht recipe. Now afraid of microphones.
Accidentally uploaded the sound of frying potatoes instead of an album. Became popular in narrow gourmet circles.
His bass is so low only whales and downstairs neighbors can hear it. Requires lots of free memory.
Stuck in 2007. Still trying to send his track via infrared port. Very loyal, but slow.
Found in the Windows Recycle Bin. Lay there for three years but never lost faith that he's a hit.
His music sounds like a 404 error you want to cry to. Perfect for existential crisis memes.
A post-punk artist who is too cheerful for his genre. Was banished from the garage for smiling.
A rapper who accidentally rhymed 'love' and 'dove' three times in one verse. Currently in rehab.
A metalhead who secretly wants to play ukulele. Needs understanding and soft pillows.
An electronic musician who is afraid of sockets. Plays on a calculator and hope.
badges: Urge to dance in an elevator
statuses: Looking for a cozy Reel or at least a Short
Doesn't bite, but might get stuck in your head
Responds to 'Oh, I know this song!'
Ready to be the background for your aesthetic life
Dreams of a million views, but okay with a like from mom
Searching for a home without AI
In search of the meaning of life and good bass
Found in the trash, but not giving up
Loves being listened to in headphones
diets: Prefers Reels under aesthetic coffee videos. Digests aggressive editing poorly.
Feeds exclusively on TikTok transitions. Loves when people change outfits to his beat.
Diet: 15-second cat clips. Capybara memes for dessert.
Loves night stories from a car window. Categorically cannot stand daylight.
Diet: makeup tutorials and unboxings. Afraid of laundry soap.
Loves productivity blogs. Goes well with the sound of a typing keyboard.
Feeds on 2x speed versions of himself. Considers it fitness.
Prefers YouTube travel vlogs. Dreams of seeing Bali at least in the background.
Digests hate in comments poorly. Requires only fire emojis.
Best consumed with extreme sports videos. Caution: may cause motion sickness.
Diet: long podcasts about the meaning of life. Loves being turned down low.
Loves fried memes from 2012. Feeds on nostalgia and pixels.
Feeds on Minecraft streams. Loves the sound of breaking blocks.
Prefers workout stories. Dreams of seeing your six-pack.
Goes well with cooking videos. Especially with the crunch of the crust.
Diet: life hacks that never work. Feeds on irony.
Loves drawing timelapses. Loves the smell of digital paint.
Feeds on vintage tech unboxings. Enjoys the smell of 90s plastic.
Loves dance challenges for people over 30. Very understanding.
Prefers Shorts with space facts. Feels like a star.

footer.description: The world's first digital shelter for artists deactivated by algorithms. We believe every track deserves a second chance.

about.title: Who Are We?
about.description-1: We are volunteers who can't stand seeing AI algorithms cast out thousands of talented musicians just because they didn't hit a 15-second trend.
about.description-2: In our shelter, artists get clean clouds, regular listening, and hope to find their content maker. We don't take commissions—only likes.

partner.label: OUR PARTNER
partner.title: Where do they live in the wild?
partner.description: When our proteges get stronger, they move to bilbo. It's not just Telegram/MAX streaming; it's an ecosystem where music is filtered by real people, not soulless scripts.
partner.achieve-3-3: Here, an artist is a person, not a database row.

form.title: Drop Off an Artist
form.description-1: Found a forgotten track on an old USB? Put it in the box, and we'll find them a family.

description.title: What's the trouble?
description.placeholder: e.g., Out of tune but really wants to be on TikTok

artist.attention: * Be careful when following links: the artist may start begging for likes and comments.
artist.attention-2: By clicking the button, you promise not to apply 200% bass boost.

contacts.subtitle: “I've survived three playlist removals myself; I know every ward by their beats.”
contacts.silence: If I don't answer, I'm either walking new artists or arguing with the Yandex algorithm. Please don't throw slippers at me.

artists.description: They are waiting for you to add them to your next meme or video.

visits.title: Raising for 'Vitamins'
visits.description: All donations go toward server costs and buying plugins for homeless artists.

form.title: Send a Parcel
form.hash: Transaction Hash (so we can find your transfer)

artist.title: Who are we warming up?
artist.placeholder: Start typing name or ID (e.g. Tyuf...)

visits.attention-1: * Warning: Administration is not responsible for funds spent.
visits.attention-2: All artists are spiritually full but physically hungry.

User manual included
Slightly melancholic on Thursdays

The most affectionate in its genre
Fully digitally disinfected

Your personal soundtrack for a grocery run
